title: Thawing and freezing cell stocks

steps: Thaw cells using bead bath or warmed autoclaved water until there is only a small ice crystal left DMSO is toxic to cells at high concentrations, so it is important to thaw quickly
Add a few drops of warmed media to complete thawing
Transfer cells to a T75 with 12ml pre-warmed media We thaw into a T75 so that DMSO concentration is <1%
Change media the next day to remove DMSO
Trypsinise at late log phase (approaching confluence) following subculturing protocol
After quenching trypsinised cells with media, perform a cell count using trypan blue exclusion
Aliquot appropriate number of cells, spin down 300g 5 min e.g over 25 million cells for 10x aliquots of 2.5 million
Resuspend cells in freezing media (30% L media, 60% dFBS, 10% DMSO) to required concentration for 1 ml aliquots. E.g 2.5 million cells/ml, suitable for thawing in a T75
Transfer the vials to Mr Frosty (chamber must be filled with isopropanol) This is to decrease temperature by 1ºC per minute to preserve cells during the freezing process
Store at -80ºC overnight, then remove from Mr Frosty and transfer to liquid nitrogen storage. Update liquid nitrogen storage map.
Obtain cells from liquid nitrogen, keeping them on dry ice until ready to thaw. Update liquid nitrogen storage map after thawing.
creator: Victoria Munro
